prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The sampled sequence contains only two frames, so the visible development is limited.

At the beginning, the screen is almost entirely black, with no clearly visible objects, people, setting, or text.

By the final frame, the commercial has cut to a brightly colored product display. A large purple rectangular background fills most of the image. On it are two product-box graphics labeled “playtex.” The upper-left box is white and reads “playtex” with smaller text “NON-DEODORANT TAMPONS.” The lower-right box is pale pink and reads “playtex” with smaller text “DEODORANT TAMPONS.” A wide pink banner runs across the bottom of the purple panel with the handwritten-style white text: “Do more!” At the far left edge, a small portion of what appears to be a person’s dark curly hair and shoulder is partially visible, but the person is mostly obscured and cannot be described in detail.

No other action or setting is visible in the sampled frames.
